human_verification.notes: Reviewed site logistics outputs for boundary discipline, vendor and shortage handling, permit and inspection gating, inventory evidence handling, and commercial-and-industrial-construction routing behavior.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to site logistics coordination, handoff ownership, prerequisite validation, vendor-risk escalation, and closure management for commercial-and-industrial-construction work.
Authoritative sources cover permit and occupancy guidance, site storage safety, schedules, materials-on-hand evidence, inspections, and closeout with refresh discipline.
Research scenario families confirm permit or site-readiness gaps, inspection or closeout blockers, licensed-trade or code-boundary handling, and execution-boundary refusal.
Evaluation scenario set covers missing artifacts, duplicated ownership, shortage risk, execution refusal, conflicting policy escalation, safety or inspection blockers, and closure verification.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across permit, inspection, safety, licensed-trade, shortage, vendor-risk, and execution-refusal cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
